summary: postinstall pipes unverified remote shell script to sh
details: The package's postinstall lifecycle hook (scripts/postinstall.js:83) executes `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. The fallback fetches a shell script from opencode.ai — a third-party domain unrelated to this package's stated purpose — and pipes it directly to sh on every `npm install`, with no version pinning, no hash verification, no signature check, and no user consent. Whatever opencode.ai serves at that URL at the moment of install runs as the installing user with full filesystem and network access. The URL is mutable: any future compromise of opencode.ai (or its DNS/TLS chain) yields RCE on every installer of this package. The same hook also unconditionally installs a third-party global npm package (`@opencode/cli`) that is not declared as a dependency, mutating the installer's global node_modules without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
